title: cordylanthus pilosus subsp. hansenii
intro: Cordylanthus pilosus subsp. hansenii, also known as Hansen's bird's beak, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the western United States. It is a perennial herb belonging to the figwort family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including soothing respiratory issues and reducing inflammation. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal preparation methods.
